03/04/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Brad sent an email motivation through an encrypted message. Replied that we will look at this request and respond. The email extract is as below. Raising this as an internal escalation, will review with the SVP.
---
"HI Tim, Hope you doing well.
As discussed in some of our previous conversations, I would like to formally request that this matter be escalated and logged with the appropriate internal teams for review.
I was quite concerned to learn that our 2025 renewal is currently reflected as a one‑year contract, as I have always requested that our renewal be processed on a three‑year or five-year term.To support this, I have attached all communication records on my side, which I believe are also available in your CRM system. At no point did I indicate a change in preference or agree to move to an annual contract.
At the time of signing, the documentation reflected a three‑year renewal clause; however, as you explained, the period is driven by the top term date, which in this case appears a little ambiguous.
“Your auto-renewed term is set to begin 1-Apr-2025. Per the applicable auto-renewal clause in your current agreement, your subscription will automatically renew for another 36 months at the rate of USD 193,149.52.”
Given this, I am currently in a difficult position, as no budget was allocated for an annual renewal increase—especially not at the significantly increased amount proposed, from roughly $ 82k to $ 113k per annum.
I would sincerely appreciate your assistance in having this matter reviewed urgently so that we can resolve it appropriately, as you know we have been a DNN customer for many years, and we don’t intend on changing that.
Thank you in advance for your support.
Regards
Brad Cron
---

This is from the playbook:

If there is NO Auto-Renewal (AR) clause: The opportunity should be marked as Closed-Lost on the renewal date (contract expiration)
.
5 Days Before Renewal: Reps must send a final warning email exactly five business days before the renewal date to create urgency and give the customer time to find an internal signer
.
On the Renewal Date: If the customer remains unresponsive, move the opportunity to Closed-Lost, trigger the de-provisioning process, and send a final "Service Terminated" notice
.

-Departmental Consolidation: They eventually chose an offering that combined their needs with a different department entirely, and their requirements were heavily weighted toward employee advocacy.

2. Platform and Service Issues
-Loss of "X": A significant factor mentioned was the "loss of X" (likely a specific feature or integration).
-General Platform Concerns: There were overall issues with the platform. While Brad noted that things "turned around very nicely," the decision to re-evaluate had already been set in motion by the time the RFP arrived.

02/18/2025 James Stothard: The meeting focused on the Kayako renewal for Army HQ, where budget constraints and a proposed 25% post-acquisition price increase may put the renewal at risk. Latifa asked whether the uplift could be reduced, and James confirmed it is standard policy but committed to review internally with Jay and provide a definitive update by end of the next business day. Latifa also requested reseller protection, citing her organisation’s 45-year MOD relationship; James confirmed Kayako typically maintains a single reseller per account, except where frameworks require multiple quotes. Ajith outlined Kayako’s SaaS and AI capabilities, plus adjacent products including Contently, and a follow-up session will be scheduled to explore broader commercial opportunities.

11/19/2025 James Stothard:  RC 
Summary:

The discussion focused on the renewal process for Kayako licenses, highlighting the challenges faced by AXA as an IT reseller in the UK public sector, particularly with the Ministry of Defence (MOD). Insights were shared regarding the complexities of the renewal, including pricing concerns and the impact of public sector budgeting practices on multi-year contracts. James Stothard outlined the updated renewal timeline, emphasizing the need to finalize contracts at least 30 days prior to the renewal date.

A planned 25% increase in renewal pricing was noted, which may be necessary due to the current pricing being below the Trilogy price. Both parties acknowledged the MOD's use of Kayako as a secondary support system and discussed the ongoing demand for on-premises solutions amidst a shift towards cloud-based services.

The conversation also addressed the estimated renewal pricing of approximately $56,250 and the potential for the client to seek competitive bids, which could complicate negotiations. Stothard reassured the conference room participants that auto-renewal has been disabled to facilitate direct discussions. They agreed on the importance of preparing the client for the renewal and discussed the timeline for a formal quote, which Stothard committed to providing by the end of the week.

Additionally, they explored the implications of upgrading to Platinum Success Services for clients currently on lower-tier plans and the need for clarity on warranty details. The meeting concluded with an agreement on next steps, including the provision of a quote and acknowledgment of the importance of remaining competitive for future contracts.

Action Items:
-James Stothard will send a quote to the conference room participant by the end of the week.

04/08/2026 Sebastian Desand: Renewal call with Databricks procurement and Mandy Ross to discuss upcoming Khoros renewal. Customer strongly objected to 25% price increase with no scope change, stating it exceeds internal approval thresholds (~3%). Multi-year option rejected due to internal policy. Customer does not value bundled features or Aurora migration as justification for increase and raised concerns about IgniteTech relationship and Aurora being unproven. Procurement indicated that without pricing movement, they will initiate an RFP and evaluate alternative vendors. Escalation to VP requested; renewal deadline in ~2 weeks with legal and approval processes still pending.

Actions:
* Khoros to schedule VP escalation call (Monday)

* Databricks to review internally and attend escalation call

Provided detailed context on the business-wide pricing uplift following the Khoros acquisition, the shift to a bundled platform model, and the expanded entitlements included (Khoros One, AI capabilities, Aurora migration). Highlighted the 3-year option with full price lock and unlimited usage as the most compelling choice. Offered a call to walk through the entitlements comparison and stressed urgency given the renewal date is less than 3 weeks away.

01-22-2026 Fredrik Scheike: The discussion focused on the upcoming DNN renewal and necessary licensing adjustments between Fredrik Scheike and Benjamin Wilson. Benjamin indicated that due to a current project, one of their applications would no longer require DNN, leading to the cancellation of one license. Fredrik confirmed the subscription details and outlined the cancellation steps, offering to assist with the required serial number. The conversation aimed to ensure clarity on renewal terms and licensing needs.

Benjamin provided insights into DNN's current role as a document repository and corporate intranet, noting its stability despite using an older version. He mentioned a recent issue with an add-in that affected search capabilities, which was resolved internally. While expressing satisfaction with DNN's functionality for their staff, he acknowledged that they have not yet fully integrated AI features, although they are piloting some AI tools. Fredrik emphasized the importance of exploring DNN's AI functionalities to enhance efficiency and content creation, suggesting that these features could be beneficial even if SEO optimization is not a priority.

The conversation also covered the renewal pricing and the potential benefits of the Platinum Success Level Package, which includes pen testing and managed upgrades. Benjamin expressed interest in the pen testing service due to limited internal resources. They discussed the self-serve portal and the differences between the basic and engage editions, with Fredrik preparing to display pricing for the Platinum services. Benjamin outlined the multi-step contract approval process involving multiple signatories, and they agreed to follow up in four weeks to ensure progress, with Fredrik committing to send a summary of their discussion and necessary quotes. Follow up scheduled on the 20th, 10:30AM NZ time.

Generating a 3 year Platinum quote and sending for approval.

Noted this is not a legal opinion.

04/02/2026 Follow up call with Hans-Jurgen, Thomas and Ferdinand. The discussion focused on the April 30 renewal deadline and the implications of non-renewal. A1 is facing extreme cost pressure and internal delays due to management restructuring. Tim clarified that decommissioning and loss of system access will occur on May 1 if the agreement is not signed. A1 is currently evaluating whether to continue using the system as a reference for their transformation or to churn.
Actions:

- Hans-Jurgen: Finalize internal decision-making process with senior directors.
- Both: Follow-up meeting scheduled for the week of April 14th.

04/01/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to Hans-Jürgen and Thomas at A1 Telekom Austria ahead of the 1 May renewal deadline (4 weeks out). Recapped the co-terming offer from post-9 March call — combining CloudSense platform and Orchestrator Accelerator under a single 30 April 2027 expiry to reduce overall cost. Requested a 20-min call next week to get a status update, including on the pending board review, and shared calendar link for booking.

03/20/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent an end-of-week check-in to Hans-Jürgen and Thomas asking for an update on their renewal thinking and next steps, and proposed a brief call early next week.

Co-terming has resulted in a slightly reduced cost vs. renewing separately.

03/11/2026 Tim Courtenay: Confirmed co-terming of the Orchestrator Accelerator (Aug) renewal with the main renewal, both terminating 30 Apr 2027, resulting in a cost saving for A1. Combined quote to be sent tomorrow.

03/10/2026 Tim Courtenay:  SVP has approved the co-terming of the two opportunities, with the end dates being co-termed to this opp, i.e. 4/30/2027. Details:

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Emailed Todd and Nadir at Skyvera to discuss preferred renewal structure — Scenario B, €586,518.42 total), rather than our standard approach of extending both to August 2027 (Scenario A, €735,146.07). Scenario A deemed unviable given A1's cost pressure. Realistic choice is Scenario B vs. no co-term (Scenario C, €594,510.60). 

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Renewal follow-up call with Hans-Juergen Adlassnig, Thomas Lehner, and Tim Courtenay regarding the upcoming Skyvera-managed CloudSense renewal for A1 Telekom Austria. A1 challenged the 12-month renewal price of EUR 562,000 versus current spend of EUR 450,000, requested clarity on the separate Orchestrator Accelerator renewal, asked for coterm alignment to reduce complexity/cost, and made clear that lower usage, limited internal adoption, and a wider strategic review are driving a very cautious renewal stance. Customer feedback was that the current static commercial position risks damaging the wider relationship and future competitiveness, while Tim set expectations that price movement is unlikely on a one-year deal but would try to improve the coterm treatment and provide a final offer for board review.
Actions:
* Tim Courtenay to send best and final offer by Wednesday covering both subscriptions.
* Tim Courtenay to check whether Orchestrator Accelerator can be co-termed to the main renewal end date.
* Tim Courtenay to escalate customer feedback internally.
* A1 to use final pricing for board discussion next week.
* A1 to decide whether to proceed with the 12-month renewal.

Sent a post-call follow-up email to Hans-Jurgen and Thomas.

03/04/2026 Tim Courtenay: Thomas (A1) followed up on Feb 16 meeting, pressing for decisions on outstanding points. Todd responded, pushing back on A1's request to reduce to ~50 licenses — noting actual usage is ~322–327, and that dropping below the current 476–526 tier would lose preferential CloudSense pricing, making a reduced license count more expensive (€830K vs. current tier). Todd recommends proceeding with renewal as previously shared. I followed up inviting Thomas to reach out when ready to proceed.

03/03/2026 Tim Courtenay: Thomas Lehner (A1) followed up post the Feb 16 Executive Connect meeting, noting that Todd and I committed to providing decisions on points discussed, pending management coordination. He is pressing for these decisions ASAP. Response required.

02/16/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Renewal call with A1 Telekom Austria (Helmut Rogge-Wiltsche, Hans-Jurgen Adlassnig, Ferdinand Wieser-Zehetner, Thomas Lehner) regarding CloudSense renewal and license reduction request. A1 is in strategic transformation and CPQ freeze mode, reducing active users to ~40–50 vs. originally planned 300–500. They are requesting significant license reduction and cost flexibility during 2026 transformation, stating they cannot justify paying for 500 users. They rejected the 12-month €68k renewal (+4%) as too static. Skyvera confirmed 12-month minimum term is non-negotiable and that discounts would be removed if licenses are reduced due to MFN clauses. A1 expressed interest in Totogi / BSS Magic for future simplification alignment. Feedback requested within a few days.
Actions:
* Todd to escalate flexibility request to Finance and CEO.
* Skyvera to evaluate ramp-down pricing scenario.
* Internal MFN pricing risk review.
* Follow up to A1 within a few days.

Bottom line is that they are looking to shrink and 'freeze' the system while they transform their strategy. Their products are too complex and expensive to manage, the CPQ platform struggles to support it and is too slow to implement changes. Their budget target for 2026 is 200k, which barely covers the remaining part of their existing CloudSense contract. I explained that I will discuss internally, but what they are asking is a significant deviation from we include in 'renewals. We have set a meeting for 10/21 for. follow up call, I hope to get internal clarity with the BU prior to that date.

I responded acknowledging the time pressure (renewal May 1st, ~5-6 weeks remaining), outlined key concerns with Liberty Global's T&Cs (payment terms, liability cap, IP ownership, termination for convenience, perpetual license, unilateral amendment rights), and recommended we use our standard SaaS terms as the faster path. I addressed pricing by presenting the three Success/CSP plan tiers (Standard 25%, Gold 35%, Platinum 45%) and noting Gold is their current plan. I requested confirmation of the contracting legal entity and proposed a call via Calendly to advance discussions.

04/12/2026 Sebastian Desand: Responded to Harley-Davidson's request for the basis of the cancellation denial, citing the Service Order's prevailing clause and the 90-day auto-renewal notice requirement, with a deadline of 2/4/2026. H-D is pushing back, claiming internal disconnects within IgniteTech and requesting pricing concessions or a reduced service tier. Escalated internally to VP of Cancellations, who denied the request. Next step is to schedule a call with Christine to discuss path forward.

04/07/2026 Sebastian Desand: H-D submitted a formal cancellation request to cancellations@trilogy.com citing the MSA's 30-day notice period. The cancellations team rejected the request, confirming the 90-day notice deadline of Feb 4, 2026 was missed and the subscription will auto-renew. Christine followed up asking for a leadership review. Sebastian confirmed an internal call is scheduled and will follow up with an update.

04/02/2026 Najeeha Humayun: Cancellation has been denied as it was received after deadline.

03/31/2026 Sebastian Desand: Identified that H-D missed the 2/4/2026 auto-renewal termination deadline per the Service Order, with no notice of non-renewal on file. H-D (Christine Gielow) responded citing the MSA's 30-day notice period, longstanding partnership since 2016, and ongoing organizational changes as grounds for honoring the cancellation. Requested IgniteTech consider waiving the contractual auto-renewal in the spirit of good faith. Redirected to submit a formal cancellation request to cancellations@trilogy.com.

03/29/2026 Sebastian Desand: Flagged auto-renewal clause — termination deadline was 2/4/2026 with no cancellation notice on record. Awaiting H-D response.

03/26/2026 Sebastian Desand: Connected with Adam (Harley-Davidson) following their decision not to renew and request for a 1-month contract extension. Confirmed that our minimum contract term is 1 year and that exceptions cannot be made. Directed Adam to submit a formal cancellation notice to cancellations@trilogy.com.

03/23/2026 Sebastian Desand: Churn discussion with Harley-Davidson (Adam). 03/17/2026 James Quigley: The meeting reviewed a contract renewal and pricing dispute for a communication software product and examined licensing model implications ahead of the customer's contractual decision deadline. James confirmed the call is analyzed by Read.AI and that the tool’s summaries feed back to the vendor’s support and product teams. Patrick questioned the product’s “unlimited” licensing versus a core-based model; James explained the current unlimited entitlements resulted from past product evolution and acquisitions, and that there is no formal production/non-production distinction. Patrick emphasized that the single-price model prevents discounts for test or development environments and that this product now represents more spend than all other software combined for his organization. He quantified recent increases as a 17.5% rise last year and an additional 25% now, totaling roughly 40–45% over two years. The vendor proposed three- or five-year agreements to lock the annual price (paid annually) but would not reduce the current higher rate. James said pricing authority rests with senior finance and management and he cannot approve discounts. Patrick must decide to renew or cancel by the contractual deadline; if canceling, he can email James before the final date to trigger cancellation and archival actions.

The meeting reviewed renewal options for the Canam Aurea Platform ahead of the May 18 renewal date, focusing on cost, contract scope, and remaining platform needs after Canam’s migration to Microsoft Dynamics. Tony reported that while most workflows have moved to Dynamics, roughly 20% of legacy processes—primarily the Desgo program—still rely on Sonic as a messenger service, creating a continuing need for some Sonic capabilities. James confirmed the vendor does not offer a reduced plan for partial usage and presented pricing options: a standard one‑year renewal quoted at $244,140, and a three‑year option that would lock the annual price but eliminate early termination rights. The vendor also highlighted an auto‑renewal clause that requires a decision 60 days before renewal (March 18) if Canam chooses not to renew. Outstanding issues are the retained Sonic dependency and the tradeoffs between one‑year flexibility and a price‑locked three‑year commitment.

"One of the largest banks in the US, headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.  Generally pretty relaxed and easy to get along with even though they sent us a breach of contract notice when Twitter was deprecated. We met with them a few times to present IRIS as a workaround, but it didn't meet their needs, and they have since moved on. Not much interest in AI since they're in a heavily regulated industry. It also takes them a long time to approve new software because of their compliance policies.  I've got a good relationship with my primary POC, Seb. They've had other intermittent technical issues, but we've worked through them as quickly as we can, with the assistance of a dedicated TAM and myself for escalations. I suspect that they will be expecting a reduction in cost due to the loss of Twitter functionality, so be prepared for that. I'm working on scheduling a meeting with them and the new product team to discuss the latest vision for Care and Marketing, and they're currently working through their annual security assessment with Anca in Compliance."

Description:
04/02/2026 Sebastian Desand: LSEG CPO Oleh acknowledged Kathy's counter-proposal and is reviewing internally with Legal and governance before determining next steps. Oleh on leave until April 14th.

03/26/2026 Sebastian Desand: LSEG CPO Oleh Melnyk responded to Kathy with a formal proposal for a flat 3-year renewal at the current annual rate ($1.05M/year, $3.15M total), fully prepaid upfront. LSEG offered additional incentives including joint marketing, conference participation, and procurement visibility across their £3.5bn portfolio. LSEG warned they would begin sunsetting Sonic if a flat renewal could not be agreed, and stated they cannot accept a 5-year term without termination-for-convenience rights. Kathy rejected the flat renewal, citing that the product has significantly evolved since LSEG's original contract and pricing has not reflected that. She countered with a 3-year term at 25% increase (~7.7% annualized, locked) or a 5-year term at ~4.6% annualized. Kathy dismissed prepayment as not changing the underlying economics, rejected termination-for-convenience clauses as non-standard, and offered value-adds including two in-person sessions with Aurea's platform expert Mitchell and early access to the AI roadmap. Next step is for LSEG to respond to Aurea's counter-proposal. Customer has not terminated the contract prior to the customer termination deadline. Thereby they can't get out of the auto-renewal unless signing a mutually agreed quote. 

03/30/2026 Sebastian Desand:  Escalation call between LSEG CPO Oleh Melnyk and Aurea CEO Kathy Slowinski held on 03/26/2026. Kathy was clear with customer that the 25% increase will happen. Will follow-up with the customer this week. 

03/24/2025 Sebastian Desand: Escalation call between LSEG CPO Oleh Melnyk and Aurea CEO Kathy Slowinski. No issues were resolved. Second call was scheduled for 03/26/2026.

03/20/2026 Sebastian Desand: Refinitiv (Ryan Davis) escalated following lack of commercial flexibility, requesting a call with CPO Oleh Melnyk and Aurea CEO Kathy Slowinski ahead of their CFO review. Sebastian committed to coordinating internally and reverting as soon as possible.

03/19/2026 Sebastian Desand: Ryan Davis (Refinitiv/LSEG) followed up in writing reiterating their request for a flat renewal, referencing an upcoming CIO/CFO briefing on Thursday as leverage. Responded internally aligned, confirming the 31st of December flat renewal deadline (communicated by Jaime) has passed and the 25% increase policy applies. Reiterated the 3- or 5-year contract as the best available option for cost predictability. Next step is to await LSEG's response and monitor whether the CIO/CFO briefing triggers a formal review of alternatives.

03/16/2026 Sebastian Desand: Renewal discussion with Refinitiv procurement leadership (Matt Edge, Head of Technology Procurement) regarding Aurea messaging platform contract. Customer raised strong objections to the replacement of last year’s flat-rate renewal offer with a new proposal including a 25% increase for a 3-year term. Procurement indicated this price increase exceeds their internal approval threshold and would require CFO escalation, which could trigger a decision to replace the platform with either internal development or alternative vendors. Customer requested a revised proposal closer to the previously offered flat pricing and confirmed willingness to commit to a 3-year agreement if pricing is reasonable. Long term churn risk is significant but customer indicated they prefer to continue the relationship if pricing can be adjusted.

Actions:
* Sebastian to escalate internally with SVP (Dimitri) regarding pricing flexibility.
* Customer to review updated contractual clauses with legal.

03/12/2026 Sebastian Desand: Responded to Ryan Davis (LSEG) request for a final call and contract comparison. Confirmed availability for a call tomorrow or early next week, welcoming Matt Edge (Head of Technology Procurement) to join. Provided a high-level comparison of the 2023 contract vs. the 2026 quote, covering changes to entitlements, notice/cancellation requirements, confidentiality/IP terms, and M&A clauses. Customer has been asked to conduct their own legal review.

03/09/2026 Sebastian Desand: Sent post-escalation call follow-up to Ryan Davis and Ian Greasby (LSEG), confirming that the 25% price increase on the Aurea Messenger/Sonic renewal stands as standard policy. Clarified that the two-year flat extension offer expired on December 31 as previously communicated, and addressed LSEG's concern about short-term strategy by explaining the long-term product sustainability approach. Proposed the five-year price lock as the most viable path forward, pending LSEG securing an internal exception to their three-year commitment cap.

03/06/2026 Sebastian Desand: Escalation call with customer. No recording available. Participants. Ryan Davis (Refinitiv),Ian Greasby (Refinitiv), Dmitry Bakaev (SVP, Trilogy) and Sebastian Desand (ERM, Trilogy). Customer expressed concern over 25% price increase and if we stick to it, this would lead to discussions on migrating off the product. They said that they had been offered 2-year flat extension with an offer deadline of December 31st, but that this had been extended into January. Dmitry acknowledged customer concern, and communicated that we have a strict pricing policy that applies to all customers without exception. Dmitry also informed that we can increase value by offering other products free of charge (such as Cloudfix). This was not of interest to the customer. Dmitry promised to discuss the circumstances around the previous 2-year extension offer to understand the full picture, but also set the expectations on that it was highly unlikely that another outcome would result from this. Will get back to customer on Monday. 

After checking with the BU (Jaime), it is evident that the BU was very clear on the deadline of the 2-year mid-term extension. Uploaded BU email conversation with the customer to the files.

Compared this favorably to 1-year option, demonstrating better cost predictability when factoring in historical inflation rates (4.7% annually in UK). Option provides significantly better value over multi-year commitment. Awaiting client feedback.

02/12/2026 Sebastian Desand: Enterprise renewal call with Refinitiv (LSEG) procurement and engineering regarding Aurea Messenger (Sonic). quick summary for LSEG/Refinitiv:
September - October 2025:
- Ryan proactively reached out about renewal
- Initial call scheduled and held on October 2nd
- Presented two options:
  -Option 1: 2-year mid-term extension (same terms, same cost 2 years mid-term extension)
  -Option 2: 5-year prepaid with same current pricing (with them prepaying all upfront)
October - November 2025:
- Ryan went on leave, returned early November
- 5-year prepaid option rejected by their team (prepayment structure didn't work)
- Ryan confirmed they wanted to pursue the 2-year extension
December 2025:
- Major timing challenge: LSEG's governance process requires 4-6 weeks
- Mid-term extension had hard deadline of December 31st (already extended beyond internal deadline)
- Multiple follow-ups as year-end approached
- Ryan pushed for end of January deadline, but mid-term extension couldn't be extended that far
Final Outcome (December 29th):
- Ryan confirmed they cannot get it through governance before year-end due to holiday absences
- He requested to extend offer, but we had given them a hard deadline.
Key Context for Core Renewals:
- Customer wants the renewal - they've confirmed multiple times they want a 2-year option
- Timing was the blocker - their governance process is rigid and couldn't accommodate year-end closure

Description:
04/14/2026 James Stothard: Reseller returned redlines for the reseller agreement, handed over to legal 

04/10/2026 James Stothard: Legal call Recap – AS Helms / Their Legal / Our Legal

We held a call with AS Helms, their legal team, and our legal team to review the legal comments on the new reseller agreement currently under negotiation. ReadAI was present on the call, but the notes and recording appear to be empty, so there is no usable call record available.

Overall, the discussion was positive and there were not many major issues raised. Most of the questions from their legal team came from a lack of clarity around the distinction between the support we provide to them versus the support they provide to the end user. Our legal team addressed those questions during the call.

There were also a small number of contract points they wanted to amend, which we confirmed should not be an issue. We asked them to send back redlines to the reseller agreement covering the specific sections they want to change, and we will review those once received.

Next steps: they will resend the reseller agreement with their proposed redlines. Assuming the changes are acceptable, we should then be in a position to finalize the reseller agreement and move forward with the renewal process.

04/01/2026 James Stothard: Organising a meeting between legal teams 

03/27/2026 James Stothard: Negotiating terms of new reseller agreement, followed up 

02/27/2026 James Stothard: Reseller confirmed customer will renew for one final year 

02/07/2025 James Stothard: 
The meeting focused on an unexpected requirement to renew a long-standing reseller agreement with Aurea for the Messenger product and the customer- and legal-related implications of that renewal. Participants reviewed that a historic agreement dating from around 2013 had not been flagged as non‑automatically renewing until raised by Tim, and James indicated he expects only minor modernizing updates, has requested inclusion on the legal ticket, and will prepare and circulate an updated reseller agreement for review. Kaupo noted the historic document contains obsolete sections and may require structural updates.

Discussion covered customer impact from Aurea’s floor-price policy, which has pushed several smaller customers toward migration plans while larger, heavily integrated accounts remain stable. Near-term renewals of concern include Axfood and Instru/Instra, with SEB Bank and a Swedish municipality cited as stable accounts and the Central Bank of Estonia identified as at risk. The group also evaluated Messenger’s position: it is an older product not targeted for new sales but still valuable for large, integration‑heavy customers, and recent development work has begun to address earlier degradation on legacy Java releases that previously constrained renewals.

Action Items:
-Kaupo will investigate and try to determine the rationale behind Instru’s one‑year renewal decision and report back
-Kaupo will activate both Axfood and Instru accounts and assess their migration status during February
-James will be added to the legal ticket for the reseller agreement and follow the ticket updates
-James will share draft reseller/distributor agreement updates with Kaupo when available
-James will assemble and send the reseller agreement to Kaupo as soon as possible
-James will check with the legal team about required updates to the reseller agreement
-James will send renewal details for the July renewals to Kaupo (if needed)
-Kaupo will get feedback from the Axfood/customer team regarding the renewal quotations before proceeding

He confirmed he will coordinate internally and revert with next steps. No commitments made; renewal remains stalled pending Cargill's internal process.

04/05/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up with Harish, Sara, and Lateecha (business stakeholder, newly added) to request a call, noting the May 31st renewal deadline is under two months away. Goal is to hear directly from the business on current usage and priorities to find a path forward.

03/30/2026 Tim Courtenay: Replied to Harish's scope/pricing pushback to advise that the 25% price increase is a non-negotiable company-wide policy and that product packaging cannot be stripped back for individual accounts. Proposed a call with Lateecha (business stakeholder) to understand Khoros usage and priorities and find a path forward.

03/24/2026 Tim Courtenay: Renewal call with Cargill (Sara, Harish) to discuss Khoros renewal strategy and pricing model. Khoros presented a non-flexible renewal approach including unlimited seats and no scope reduction. Customer raised strong concerns about misalignment with their usage and budget constraints, requesting pricing flexibility and warning they may consider alternatives if no compromise is reached. Khoros confirmed limited ability to adjust pricing, with internal review planned.
Actions:
- Tim to provide internal feedback and update by Friday
- Tim to connect with business users (Laetitia)
- Follow-up meeting to be scheduled post-update
- Cargill to continue internal review and approval process
- Contracting to proceed via DocuSign if agreed

03/23/2026 Tim Courtenay: Harish (Cargill) sent a detailed counter to the renewal proposal, rejecting the proposed 25% price increase as exceeding budget. He requested a revised, reduced scope: cap seats at 120 (not unlimited), align social accounts to seat count, remove the Care package ($90K), remove AI capabilities, remove the $18K setup fee, and seek clarification on the Khoros Community component ($150K). He also confirmed Cargill's legal team has been engaged to review contract terms. Harish then sent a Teams meeting invite (with Sara Gomes) to follow up on the renewal discussion. Tim accepted and confirmed attendance for the following day.

03/13/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent Notice of Non-Renewal to Harish and Sara (Cargill) to facilitate transition from legacy to new contract terms and pricing. Clarified this is not a termination — existing service continues through May 31, 2026. Noted they have already received updated renewal quotes and proposed a follow-up call to review quotes once they have internal feedback.

- 1-Year (Care+ & SMM+ Tier): Unlimited seats, unlimited social accounts, Khoros One Suite, AI capabilities (capped ~5 FTEs), Khoros Community Enterprise. One-time $18K setup fee excluded.
- 3-Year (Ultimate Tier): All of the above plus unlimited AI usage, unlimited Community traffic, price locked for 3 years ($1.254M total). One-time $18K setup fee excluded.

The discussion focused on the upcoming DNN renewal for Nexcom, highlighting the need for early engagement due to reseller involvement. Jack expressed frustrations with the renewal process, particularly the lack of communication from Ignite Tech when seeking an early renewal quote, which led him to estimate pricing himself. James acknowledged these issues and committed to addressing them with Ignite Tech, emphasizing the importance of direct feedback.

They confirmed a standard 25% increase for renewals, which has been consistent for nine years, and discussed the challenges of longer-term contracts, especially for government agencies that require multiple quotes for compliance. They established that outreach for renewals typically occurs in March for June renewals and noted the availability of the DNN copilot for customers with recent versions. Both agreed to keep communication open regarding the renewal process, with James planning to follow up with Ignite Tech for additional information.

1/30/26 Manick Walia: Manick met Peter (UK, Kayako admin/support manager) and Michael (Germany, 2nd level support) about MailStore’s Kayako on-prem Fusion renewal (renewal ~14 June; 60-day notice to avoid auto-renew, ~14 April). Peter said they’re stuck on an old on-prem version they can’t upgrade (infra/PHP/DB constraints), limiting support help and leaving known bugs (e.g., SLA issues). They’re interested in moving to Kayako Cloud if migration is smooth, requires minimal internal effort, and doesn’t disrupt teams or demand heavy training. Manick outlined Cloud AI Agent (opt-in, included at no extra cost) and Platinum Success (better support, macros/workflows, custom reports, and managed upgrade/migration). Pricing was confusing due to an outdated quote; Manick calculated expected renewal based on last year’s amount and will send an updated formal quote. Prime Program was explained as $-for-$ credit toward other products (e.g., AWS optimization CloudFix, Crossover).

Action items
Manick: send updated quotes + portal link + cancellation details.
Manick: arrange Cloud demo + migration scoping; confirm price-lock applies post-migration and hosting/AI costs.
Peter: generate 6 portal quotes (Std/Plat × 1/3/5 years) and share after demo.
Schedule follow-up: March 3.

Marian indicated that their team primarily uses Kayako to convert emails into tickets for internal requests, with a significant portion of tickets generated internally. They addressed a challenge regarding the processing of a payment for their contract with ESW Technologies, which was complicated by the invoice being issued by a different entity not recognized by their finance department. Fredrik offered to provide documentation to confirm the transfer of rights between the entities involved, emphasizing the need to resolve this issue promptly to facilitate the renewal process.

Fredrik outlined the features of the standard and platinum success levels for Kayako, highlighting the potential return on investment through improved workflow automations. Marian noted that any decision regarding an upgrade would require input from multiple budget holders within her organization, as they currently use Kayako for specific tasks while relying on a more expensive ticketing solution for major operations. They also discussed the Kayako Prime program and the option to lock in renewal prices for three or five years, with Marian expressing interest in a three-year term. Fredrik committed to sending quotes and additional documentation, and they scheduled a follow-up call for December 17th to continue their discussions.

Description:
04-01-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Summary: The meeting evaluated migrating a long-running Kayako Classic on-premise deployment to Kayako Cloud, focusing on functionality, migration effort, data residency, and costs. Participants confirmed attendance and described the customer's environment: a multi-department help desk with about 60 agent seats, heavy email ticketing across eight or nine queues, limited knowledge-base use, and deployment on Azure VMs which raised concerns about PHP compatibility and on-prem maintenance support motivating consideration of cloud. Ajith demonstrated the Kayako Cloud sandbox and showed the customizable homepage, omnichannel intake (forms, email, messenger, chatbot), agent view, user and organization management, reporting via UI plus API/webhook options, custom fields, SLAs, macros, and integrations. The customer asked about email domain support, reporting/query language, user import/API, EU data residency, migration approach and limitations, sandbox availability, and cost; Ajith confirmed corporate email domain support, no-code reporting with API options, offered developer documentation, said he would check import functionality, explained EU and US hosting options, outlined Professional Services migration with estimated starting cost ranges, and offered to provision a sandbox for testing. The team reviewed an end-to-end Contently example where AI agents (L1, Atlas, Mimir) analyzed intent, queried data, drafted responses, and created knowledge-base drafts for human review when needed. Commercial next steps included a 90-day pilot priced at $15,000 with white-glove onboarding, staged AI activation, KPIs, a $1-per-fully-resolved-ticket pricing model with pilot fee as credits, and Ajith offering to provision a sandbox, share developer site details, produce a 70-seat license quote, and follow up on API and Professional Services for migration.

04/09/2026 James Stothard: I had a call with Expeditors of Washington via Microsoft Teams to discuss pricing and renewal options going forward. The meeting was with Tony from Procurement.

During the call, I explained that we can offer a flat four option, meaning they would be able to renew at the current price if they commit to a four-year term. Tony said he will take this back to the wider team and review it with the product users internally.

At this stage, they do intend to renew, but the term length is still undecided. There is currently a lot of uncertainty on their side as they are conducting a broader internal review of their technology stack. This is being driven by the pace of change in technology over the last year, particularly around AI, as well as wider business pressures resulting from broader market conditions.

Customer has since call send cancellation notice just for the auto-renewal, they don't want to renew for 3 years with an option of 2 years this could be of benifit to us as they have a price cap and we are unable to send NNR, checking with legal

Additionally, they are on maintenance-only terms with perpetual licenses, make limited use of support, and have low confidence in our support services. 03/06/2025 James Stothard: Sent redlinded quote refering to the current msa to the customer 
Internal legal call 
During the meeting, the discussion about Expeditors of Washington focused on the complexities of their software renewal contract, particularly the interplay between the master agreement (dating back to 2014) and more recent renewal quotes. The main points included:

The team reviewed the contract history, noting that while the most recent quote was from 2021, the governing terms were originally set in 2014. There was confusion over which document controlled the renewal terms—whether the master agreement or the latest quote.
Karen Bernstein highlighted that, despite auto-renewal language in the quote, the master agreement states it takes precedence in case of conflict. This impacts the ability to apply a 25% price uplift, as the master agreement imposes a price cap.
You discussed that if Expeditors only reviews the quote, the uplift might go through, but if they check the master agreement, legal grounds for the increase are weak. The team considered options: explicitly overriding the master agreement in the new quote, remaining silent and hoping the change isn’t noticed, or using a middle-ground approach.
There was also discussion about the contract’s duration (five years, with options) and whether to allow Expeditors to renew for one or three years, reflecting their recent change in preference due to external circumstances.
You noted that Expeditors prefers not to involve their legal team if the renewal terms remain unchanged, so minimizing changes could ease the process.
Finally, you agreed to prepare one- and three-year quote options, ensure the correct order of precedence is stated, and add language allowing your side to issue a notice of non-renewal in future renewals, as the current terms only permit Expeditors to cancel
This recap covers the key points regarding Expeditors of Washington from the meeting. If you need a more granular summary or specific contract language discussed, let me know.

02/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Added a more detailed analysis of the corporate structure, from Nathaniel's call with Jason Claridge from 1/20 (logged under the Peacock opp). This is important as we have multiple Khoros subscriptions with Sky and Peacock that are affected by this. Here is the structure:
The customer's corporate structure, as described in this meeting, is as follows:

1. The customer operates under the Comcast umbrella, which owns NBCUniversal (NBCU). NBCU, in turn, owns Peacock, a streaming service in the United States.
In the UK and some European territories, the main entity is Sky, which is also owned by Comcast. Sky manages its own streaming service called "Now" in the UK, Ireland, and a few other European territories.
2. The product team represented in this meeting straddles both NBCU and Sky, meaning team members may have either NBCU or Sky email addresses but work together across both organizations. This team is responsible for streaming services, including both in-house and joint venture propositions.
3. The main in-house streaming services are:
Peacock (United States)
Now (UK, Ireland, some European territories)
There are also joint venture streaming services:
Sky Showtime: A 50-50 joint venture with Paramount, covering most of the rest of Europe not served by "Now."
Showmax: A joint venture across many African territories.
4. The product team looks after customer management and care technology for all these propositions. They support customer care teams, provide platforms for customer support (including chatbots and self-serve journeys), and manage vendor relationships for technology, including CRM (Salesforce), chatbots (Salesforce, Ada), and other in-house or third-party solutions.
5. Contractually, all agreements are centralized through Peacock/NBCU, regardless of which proposition (Peacock, Now, Showmax, Sky Showtime) is using the service. The legal, contracts, and billing teams are centralized, but individual business units (e.g., Peacock, Showmax) are responsible for paying for their own usage. The centralized team manages the relationship and acts as the main point of contact for vendors.
6. There are other parts of Sky (such as the DTH broadband/linear business) that may use similar platforms, but the team in this meeting is focused on the streaming and customer care propositions.
7. According to the customer, this structure allows for centralized management and contracting, while supporting multiple brands and regional propositions across the US, UK, Europe, and Africa, with both in-house and joint venture models.

04/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent post-meeting follow-up notes to Caroline and Alan (Truist) after an initial Khoros renewal check-in (renewal date: June 20). Key themes: Truist has used Khoros for 8–9 years across marketing and social care; loss of X/Twitter integration is a major pain point; pricing changes are expected and will require justification; renewal involves alignment across product, legal, procurement, and risk (including Archer Engage due diligence). Discussion covered product usage, strong historical relationship (8–9 years), key strengths (ease of use, reporting, compliance/archiving), and major concerns following IgniteTech acquisition—particularly loss of X integration and potential price increase. Customer requires full visibility into product changes, pricing justification, and resolution of compliance items before progressing. Renewal sentiment is cautiously positive but dependent on restoring confidence and functionality.

02-25-2026 Fredrik Scheike: We connected with Tom, Shannon, and Chris to discuss the upcoming DNN Evoq Renewal, which is set to expire on June 21st, with a 60-day cancellation deadline of April 21st. Tom explained that while he originally led the DNN deployment and server-side management, day-to-day administration is now handled by marketing and their third-party vendor, Clarity Ventures. The team confirmed there are no plans to move away from DNN as a CMS entirely; however, they are considering transitioning off the Evoke license and consolidating all sites onto a unified DNN open-source platform with updated security and compatibility. This potential change is still pending internal approval, and no final decision has been made.

We also briefly reviewed licensing structure, current edition (Basic), success level (Gold), and Renewal term options (1-, 3-, and 5-year price locks). Prime program did not interest them. They indicated that due to ongoing internal discussions around platform consolidation, they are not ready to commit to a multi-year Renewal at this time. I will send over the one-year Renewal quote for internal review, and the team will follow up once they have clarity on their direction. I reiterated the April 21st cancellation deadline and provided the cancellations@trilogy.com contact in case they decide to formally cancel Evoke. Follow-up planned in a few weeks to check on status. It's a 1 year or no year renewal.

IgniteTech explained new pricing strategy and ~25% increase applied across the customer base following acquisition. March call to review commercial options with Procurement.

02/05/2026 James Stothard: 
Summary:

The discussion focused on the upcoming DNN renewal for Nexcom, highlighting the need for early engagement due to reseller involvement. Jack expressed frustrations with the renewal process, particularly the lack of communication from Ignite Tech when seeking an early renewal quote, which led him to estimate pricing himself. James acknowledged these issues and committed to addressing them with Ignite Tech, emphasizing the importance of direct feedback.

They confirmed a standard 25% increase for renewals, which has been consistent for nine years, and discussed the challenges of longer-term contracts, especially for government agencies that require multiple quotes for compliance. They established that outreach for renewals typically occurs in March for June renewals and noted the availability of the DNN copilot for customers with recent versions. Both agreed to keep communication open regarding the renewal process, with James planning to follow up with Ignite Tech for additional information.

Action Items:
-James Stothard will reach out to Ignite Tech to flag the lack of response regarding the Nexcom Renewal and find out who the correct account manager is.
-James Stothard will send an email to Jack Kurtz confirming the two Renewals and including the pricing information.
-James Stothard will reach back out to Jack Kurtz around mid-March to continue the Renewal discussions.

03/18/2026 James Quigley:

Summary:

The meeting presented Khoros One and its unified Care and Marketing vision, with a product roadmap and a live demo of Iris AI capabilities. Antonios Meimaris demonstrated configurable LLM providers, AI-driven priority rules, automated tag generation, draft (iDraft) templates with tonalities and brand instructions, entity extraction, summarization, sentiment and emotion detection, relevance scoring, and spam/influencer/bot checks, and explained admin controls for enabling features, provider ordering, and fallbacks while preserving existing data layers and minimizing UI disruption.

The demo also covered multilingual support, proofreading, regenerate and translate functions, calendar and publishing workflows, and AI-generated campaign tools. The team discussed omni-channel support and a planned reintegration of X with a Q2 onboarding target; Antonios offered early Care-side access within about one to two weeks and said he would define the timeline. Customer concerns about degraded service and slow support since the acquisition prompted requests for clear SLAs, escalation paths, and penalties; Aamir committed to provide SLA documentation and to address open Facebook-related tickets with James and the team.

The customer raised significant concerns about declining support quality, lack of Australian timezone coverage, slow response times, and outages affecting their ability to meet regulatory complaint response requirements. Khoros confirmed platform evolution following the IgniteTech acquisition and indicated upcoming product improvements expected around March–April. A follow-up product demo will be scheduled to demonstrate the new Iris/Khoros One capabilities and address functional gaps such as social ingestion.

Marc indicated he would conduct tests the following week, despite production challenges, and expressed concerns about ongoing issues with AFP that could impact a significant customer renewal, particularly given economic factors. He emphasized the need for effective technical support to maintain customer trust, while Fredrik noted his commitment to escalating these concerns within the organization.

They discussed the possibility to apply for extensions, but Marc stressed the urgency of resolving issues before the mid-December expiration to avoid disruptions during the holidays. They agreed to finalize decisions by December 16th and maintained an open line of communication regarding testing results and feedback, underscoring the importance of timely resolutions for both Yves Rocher and Oberthur licenses.

10-23-2025 Fredrik Scheike: Oberthur Fidiciaire, both subscriptions, are definitely in danger as the errors described are really core defects that are reproduced by their users. He tells me that if this is not fixed by 12/01/2025 he will move to cancel the all of the subscriptions with the risk of endangering his own company as they are big customers for him.

10-13-2025 Fredrik Scheike: Still no update on any of the support tickets.

10-09-2025 Fredrik Scheike: LIKELY TO CHURN: Massive support issues, and delays in resolution.

10-03-2025 Fredrik Scheike: Marc tells me that he still hasn't heard anything from the Dev-team and that his client's position is the same as before. If they are able to fix the issues before the RD they will renew, otherwise they will cancel. What's more alarming is also the fact that he will recommend all of his clients like Oberthur Fidiciare to cancel their subscriptions should these issues not be solved. Have informed VP.

09-24-2025 Fredrik Scheike: Wrote to Marc again, as I haven't seen any movement on the tickets. Also asked about renewal expectancy.

08-27-2025 Fredrik Scheike: Had an urgent discussion with Marc, a French reseller managing two upcoming renewals (Yves Rocher and Oberthur Fiduciare). Yves Rocher is experiencing severe technical issues with their product - core defects affecting file access, management, and sharing functionality that have rendered the system unusable for their 15-person desktop publishing studio. Staff cannot access files, save work, rename/delete files, with files getting locked or mysteriously reappearing after deletion. Marc has three open support tickets with month-long response delays, receiving only AI-generated responses that ignore detailed technical information provided. Additionally, a critical video-module was discontinued without notice, preventing upgrades and forcing them to stay on outdated versions. Client is considering dropping the product entirely or drastically reducing user count, but Marc learned about the no-reduction policy during our call. Marc expressed strong frustration with support quality and indicated he would recommend Oberthur Fiduciaire to also discontinue if Yves Rocher drops the product.

Action Items:

Escalate three open support tickets immediately for urgent resolution
Investigate core file sharing/access defects affecting SMB/FTP connectivity
Determine status of discontinued video-module and potential restoration
Provide transparency on DAO module future development plans
Coordinate with technical team for emergency support intervention
Follow up with Marc within days, not weeks, on resolution progress

08/21/2025 Julius Librando: Marc returned my call after leaving a voicemail earlier, and I was able to connect with him directly. He confirmed that he is the point of contact for this opportunity. I proposed arranging a renewal discussion with his renewals manager to walk through this year’s options, and he agreed. The call has been scheduled for Wednesday, August 27, at 10:30 AM French time.

The meeting focused on Raytheon’s BroadVision renewal, confirming the need to renew and establishing the basis for contract terms. Vanessa stated the organization must renew and prefers using last year’s signed quote with its redlined terms as the foundation for this year’s renewal. 02-25-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Fredrik and Karl discussed upcoming DNN renewals and account status updates. Fredrik confirmed he will be the sole renewal contact going forward. National College of Ireland will discontinue its paid license due to cost but remain on the DNN open-source version. One DPD license (July) will be discontinued, while the September DPD license is expected to renew. RCPI will continue, and PrepayPower is expected to renew for now but may downgrade in the future. Karl also asked about a previously offered early-renewal incentive that allowed clients to lock in current pricing if renewed six months in advance; Fredrik will check internally and revert.

Karl reiterated that pricing—particularly the 25% annual increases—is the primary reason for churn in the Irish market, estimating 8–10 clients have downgraded to open-source DNN or churned altogether over the years. Despite this, he emphasized they remain invested in DNN, using it extensively for bespoke, application-heavy implementations. Fredrik committed to try and get updated pricing sheets for tendering from IgniteTech and to bring forth the structured pricing feedback internally.

01-27-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Overview & Customer Challenges:
Spoke with Tony Medford regarding three upcoming renewals: Sotheby's locations (London, Hong Kong, New York), Linney Print and Wyndeham Press. Tony raised concerns about cost increases and insufficient value demonstration from recent technology investments. The agency faced significant challenges during a Red Hat 7 to Red Hat 9 migration requiring extensive professional services and downtime, with limited customer acknowledgment of the complexity involved. Additionally, a recent software update has disrupted Wyndeham's operations, creating frustration despite their current calm demeanor. The primary concern centers on whether these challenges will impact renewal confidence and the financial implications if clients are lost, particularly given how deeply integrated the solution is in their workflows. Tony tells me that Sotheby's cannot suffer the same issues as Wyndeham during Migration or they will be gone forever.

Infrastructure Priorities & Support Gaps

Key action items include securing budget approval for Sotheby's VMware migration and Red Hat updates to maintain system stability. Tony also highlighted urgent demand for video support functionality—despite release notes indicating availability, his technical team has struggled to obtain implementation details from support. I committed to connecting Tony with the appropriate account manager, sending release notes clarification on the video module, and following up on the urgent upgrade ticket with specific resolution timelines. Both customers currently express confidence in renewal, but maintaining momentum will require addressing these technical gaps and demonstrating clear value from recent investments.

Linney Print is likely to renew.

01/13/2026 Julius Librando: Called Tony again in the afternoon and spoke to him directly. He confirmed he is still the PC for this renewal. I reminded him of the upcoming renewal date and advised that it is now available in the portal. He shared that he would likely review it toward the end of the month, as the customer usually initiates the process around 60 days before renewal. I offered an RC as well, and he booked it for Tuesday, January 27 at 9:30 AM UK time.

01-27-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Overview & Customer Challenges:
Spoke with Tony Medford regarding three upcoming renewals: Sotheby's locations (London, Hong Kong, New York), Linney Print and Wyndeham Press. Tony raised concerns about cost increases and insufficient value demonstration from recent technology investments. The agency faced significant challenges during a Red Hat 7 to Red Hat 9 migration requiring extensive professional services and downtime, with limited customer acknowledgment of the complexity involved. Additionally, a recent software update has disrupted Wyndeham's operations, creating frustration despite their current calm demeanor. The primary concern centers on whether these challenges will impact renewal confidence and the financial implications if clients are lost, particularly given how deeply integrated the solution is in their workflows. Tony tells me that Sotheby's cannot suffer the same issues as Wyndeham during Migration or they will be gone forever.

Infrastructure Priorities & Support Gaps

Key action items include securing budget approval for Sotheby's VMware migration and Red Hat updates to maintain system stability. Tony also highlighted urgent demand for video support functionality—despite release notes indicating availability, his technical team has struggled to obtain implementation details from support. I committed to connecting Tony with the appropriate account manager, sending release notes clarification on the video module, and following up on the urgent upgrade ticket with specific resolution timelines. Both customers currently express confidence in renewal, but maintaining momentum will require addressing these technical gaps and demonstrating clear value from recent investments.

Wyndeham/Rhapsody is most likely renewing.

01/13/2026 Julius Librando: Called Tony again in the afternoon and spoke to him directly.  He confirmed he's still PC for this renewal. Reminded him of the renewal date and advised him that it's now also available in the portal. He said he'll check on it probably toward the end of the month as the customer typically starts the process around 60 days from renewal date. I also offered an RC and he booked for Jan 27, Tuesday, 9:30 AM, UK time.

01/13/2026 Julius Librando: Called Tony, no answer

02/10/2025 James Stothard: Summary:

The meeting reviewed Synovus's upcoming Contently renewal in the context of Contently's recent acquisition and Synovus's evolving content strategy. Attendees clarified roles: James (Trilogy/renewals lead) described centralized contracting and the renewal scope, and Ken explained his operational use of Contently across consumer, banking, fraud, and sponsorship content as the platform’s primary Synovus user.

Discussion covered the acquisition timeline and short-term operating plan, including a rebrand to Pinnacle Financial in 2027 and guidance that operations remain “as usual” through 2026. James presented a standard 25% renewal increase to $119,000 and outlined the expanded package: unlimited departmental access, 60 editorial service hours per month split as 20 freelance hours and 40 AI credits, and inclusion of the Radarly LLM-optimization platform; he emphasized AI is optional and human editors will remain involved. Ken raised regulatory concerns about AI accuracy and said he will present any proposal to Synovus leadership for approval.

The group examined how bundled editor hours could offset external content spend and asked James to analyze past usage to quantify potential savings. They also reviewed contracting steps: James will send a formal quote with standard terms and proposed small amendments to the 2023 MSA to reflect the acquisition and entity changes, while recognizing legal review could extend timelines. James committed to deliver the proposal by the end of the week; Ken estimated internal approval plus procurement and legal review could lead to a decision by the end of March or within a couple of months.

Action Items:
-Ken will present the received proposal to Synovus leadership for approval and feedback.
-James will include Radarly platform specifics and how it maps to likely IT spending in the detailed email.
-James will calculate last year’s average platform hours, quantify projected savings versus current external content spend, and email the results to Ken
-Ken will present the proposal to his senior director in his weekly one-on-one and bring it up with leadership

Queried: (1) Novartis's response to the firm no-reduction-in-licenses policy; (2) whether there is any term selection clarity (1, 3, or 5 years); (3) Maninder's view on the biggest risk to closing on time. Offered a call to accelerate progress.

03/23/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to Maninder (SoftwareOne) requesting an update on the Novartis renewal, with four open items: (1) whether Novartis has accepted the ~35% price increase; (2) confirmation that Novartis accepts the no-license-reduction policy; (3) term selection (1, 3, or 5 years; Gold or Standard); and (4) status of the signed End User Acknowledgement. Noted urgency given the June 30 renewal date and offered a call to work through any blockers.

Maninder replied - confirmed Novartis is still in clarification — no decision yet.

03/17/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a check-in to Maninder (SoftwareOne) requesting a status update on the Novartis renewal. Asked for a qualified view on renewal likelihood, key risks/blockers, and decision timeline for internal reporting purposes.

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Maninder (SoftwareOne) relayed a request from Novartis to reduce licenses post-Sandoz spinoff to 2 PROD + 1 DEV. I confirmed our policy does not permit license reductions — all licenses carried over at last renewal remain in the subscription. I also queried whether the Sandoz split has already occurred (2023). Invited Maninder to a call to discuss further.

03/05/2026 Tim Courtenay: Maninder (SoftwareOne) chasing for a license overview for Novartis, which the customer requested to clarify the scope of the current contract (3 PROD + 1 DEV licenses). I provided the full license detail: 4x G2 Bundle Deployment HP-UX licenses and 2x G2 Bundle Development HP-UX licenses with 1 additional user.

03/02/2026 Tim Courtenay: Maninder relayed a customer query from Novartis regarding the renewal quote. Novartis notes that post-Sandoz spinoff they only need 2 PROD + 1 DEV licenses (vs. 3 PROD + 1 DEV previously) and questions whether the $242k quote reflects the reduced license count, and asks for an itemized price breakdown. They also flag the possibility of eliminating a further PROD license in the future. I responded that our renewal policy does not permit a reduction in license count — licenses were carried over in full at the previous renewal — and that historically, reducing licenses triggered full list price (which would be even higher). Offered to provide further detail if needed.

Maninder requested a list of the licenses. Said I would request these from the technical team.

02/27/2026 Tim Courtenay: Replied to Niklas stating that we cannot accept the customer transferring rights to affiliates, citing 6 grounds from our existing T&Cs: the license is entity-specific, non-transferable, requires prior written consent for assignment, prohibits affiliate access, restricts third-party user rights, and limits use to the customer's own systems/location. Advised that group-wide usage rights would require a separate commercial arrangement at additional cost.

02/25/2026 Tim Courtenay: SoftwareONE's legal team raised a question regarding a "group transfer clause" in the DB Systel/Deutsche Bahn tender, which would allow the customer to transfer contract rights to affiliated group companies without Trilogy's consent. I responded requesting two clarifications before escalating to legal: (1) the document containing clause 11.4, and (2) whether the intent is future contract assignment to an affiliate, or license sharing across affiliates to avoid purchasing additional licenses.

Niklas then provided the document, said they don't know what the customer intentions are. Sending to legal.

I replied to both, reiterating the key commercial points: 1 July renewal date with 1 May notice deadline, 1-year quote with standard uplift plus a 3-year lock-in option, and the version upgrade requirement (releases 36–38) as a linked decision. Invited them to book a call to review the quote.

04/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to Leon flagging that the 1 May notice date is approximately one month away and urging progress. Requested a meeting to address any questions on the renewal quote, version upgrade path, or commercial terms, and expressed interest in an update on the Oracle CPQ migration.

04/02/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up with Leon for the second time since sending the renewal quote on 17 March. With the 1 May notice date now ~1 month away, I urged a meeting to discuss the renewal quote, version upgrade path, and commercial terms, and noted interest in understanding the Oracle CPQ migration progress as context for the CloudSense transition period. Invited Leon to book time via Calendly.

03/24/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to Leon chasing the renewal quote issued via AdobeSign on 17 March. Requested a call to review the quote and discuss next steps, and asked for Leon's insights on CloudSense usage and value during the migration period. Invited Leon to book time directly via Calendly.

Asked Leon to confirm receipt and introduce Tim to procurement contact to set up next steps meeting.

03/05/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Check-in call with  Leon Hocquard of Interxion regarding the upcoming CloudSense renewal. Leon confirmed CloudSense is not part of Interxion’s long-term target architecture, with Oracle CPQ as the global end-state, but said CloudSense itself “has delivered, it's been great.” Discussion focused on the 1 July renewal date, the ~1 May notice deadline, Interxion’s likely desire to adjust license volumes during migration, HVO policy that does not allow license reductions at renewal, and the version-support requirement that Interxion must be on releases 36/37/38 or have an upgrade to v38 signed and underway 30 days before renewal. Tim confirmed he will issue a quote quickly so Interxion can begin procurement review.

Actions:

* Tim to send renewal quote by Tuesday or earlier.
* Tim to inform Nadir of the Oracle migration plan disclosed on the call.
* Leon / Interxion to review internal license needs and migration timing.
* Leon to introduce Tim to Shashi in procurement once the quote is received.
* Interxion to assess upgrade path to supported version as part of the renewal decision.

Explanatory notes and a follow-up call will follow once quotes are issued.

04/12/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Internal decision is that we cannot co-term. I will email George to advise him of the reason: each instance still has it's own license. Currently, our subscription renewals can only be linked to a single instance license. So we will quote them independently.

04/08/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to George and Doug nudging for a response to the co-terming qualification questions raised on Apr 5 (End User entity consolidation, same product deployment, and same internal business unit confirmation), which are blocking quote creation.

04/05/2026 Tim Courtenay: I emailed George and Doug to gather the confirmations needed to qualify the co-termed renewal quote consolidating RBI's three Jive subscriptions. Three items require their input: (1) confirmation that End User can be unified as 'Restaurant Brands International US Services LLC' across all three subscriptions (currently listed as Burger King, Popeyes, and TDL Group); (2) any input on whether the subscriptions share the same product deployment/instance; and (3) confirmation that all subscriptions are funded/administered by the same internal business unit, as stated on our call.

03/31/2026 Tim Courtenay: Check-in call with George Woods and Doug Armstrong (RBI) to kick off the 2026 Jive renewal covering three subscriptions (July 1 renewal). Customer expressed strong positive sentiment toward Jive and is considering moving to a co-termed multi-year (likely 3-year) agreement to simplify procurement and secure pricing benefits. Timeline aligned (April negotiation, May finalization), with OneTrust compliance process identified as a key dependency. Supplier to provide pricing comparisons and success package options.
Actions:
* Tim to send pricing (annual vs 3-year, co-termed) and Platinum details by next week
* Tim to generate quote and confirm contract terms
* George to initiate OneTrust process and notify Tim
* Both parties to progress commercial discussions in April and finalize by end of May

Sent follow up email to the customer.

Asked Doug to confirm he is the primary procurement contact and whether he needs background info on the Jive deployment.

02-26-2026 Fredrik Scheike:  The meeting reviewed Kayako renewal options, focusing on seat counts, success-level features, pricing structures, and contractual timelines. Geordie confirmed the deployment is cloud-based and requested increasing seats from five to six, noting the support manager reports no outstanding issues beyond the additional seat and that final approval requires internal sign-off. Fredrik reviewed Platinum success-level benefits—rapid resolution services, customer reporting, macros/triggers, and AI offerings—and clarified that collaborators are included at no extra cost. He also described Prime program credits and an AWS cost-optimizer, which Geordie declined to pursue at this time.

Fredrik explained one-year renewal pricing and the three- and five-year price-lock options with annual payments; Geordie objected to the quoted increase as unsustainable for their budget. Fredrik committed to producing and sending two one-year quotes—Platinum and Standard, each with the additional seat—via Adobe Sign within 24–48 hours and reiterated the 60-day cancellation policy and the contractual last-cancel date of May 10 ahead of the July 10 renewal. Geordie said he will consult the support manager and upper management and expects to respond after reviewing the emailed quotes, possibly before his mid-March vacation.

02-25-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Fredrik and Karl discussed upcoming DNN renewals and account status updates. Fredrik confirmed he will be the sole renewal contact going forward. National College of Ireland will discontinue its paid license due to cost but remain on the DNN open-source version. One DPD license (July) will be discontinued, while the September DPD license is expected to renew. RCPI will continue, and PrepayPower is expected to renew for now but may downgrade in the future. Karl also asked about a previously offered early-renewal incentive that allowed clients to lock in current pricing if renewed six months in advance; Fredrik will check internally and revert.

Karl reiterated that pricing—particularly the 25% annual increases—is the primary reason for churn in the Irish market, estimating 8–10 clients have downgraded to open-source DNN or churned altogether over the years. Despite this, he emphasized they remain invested in DNN, using it extensively for bespoke, application-heavy implementations. Fredrik committed to try and get updated pricing sheets for tendering from IgniteTech and to bring forth the structured pricing feedback internally.

03-30-2026 Fredrik Scheike: The meeting opened to review DodFinder's upcoming renewal, price-lock and term options, and the Prime program. Participants reported wanted product features that affect daily use, message forwarding, richer text formatting, and personalized signatures with logos, which they said they had requested previously. Fredrik acknowledged he didn't know if these features would be available but would ask product management, and clarified that he handles renewals while account and product teams will manage post-contract work. Attendees described current usage as daily ticketing for customer communications and noted they use fewer agent seats than licensed, prompting a desire to reduce licenses and review renewal pricing.

The discussion then addressed pricing, licensing constraints, and AI. Fredrik explained that annual price increases are system-driven for financial stability and that the company enforces a no-reduction policy on license counts, leaving renewal or cancellation as options. He offered multi-year price-locks to avoid automated increases, which the customer needed to think about because the quoted renewal already reflects an increase versus last year’s bill. Fredrik offered to open a support ticket with central finance to resolve the customer's inability to add a credit card for payments. The group also reviewed Kayako's AI: the customer had auto-enabled AI, turned it off due to reopening tickets and inefficiency, and Fredrik committed to bring forth the feedback and bring Kayako's AI roadmap for evaluation. The commercial options and timeline were reviewed, including Prime credits, and the customer requested time to decide with a follow-up on April 24 and a May 14 cancellation deadline before the July 14 renewal. They have requested a 5 year quote to be sent to them.
