Description:
04/14/2026 James Stothard: Reseller returned redlines for the reseller agreement, handed over to legal 


04/10/2026 James Stothard: Legal call Recap – AS Helms / Their Legal / Our Legal

We held a call with AS Helms, their legal team, and our legal team to review the legal comments on the new reseller agreement currently under negotiation. ReadAI was present on the call, but the notes and recording appear to be empty, so there is no usable call record available.

Overall, the discussion was positive and there were not many major issues raised. Most of the questions from their legal team came from a lack of clarity around the distinction between the support we provide to them versus the support they provide to the end user. Our legal team addressed those questions during the call.

There were also a small number of contract points they wanted to amend, which we confirmed should not be an issue. We asked them to send back redlines to the reseller agreement covering the specific sections they want to change, and we will review those once received.

Next steps: they will resend the reseller agreement with their proposed redlines. Assuming the changes are acceptable, we should then be in a position to finalize the reseller agreement and move forward with the renewal process.

04/07/2026 James Stothard: Follow up meeting booked for 04/09

02/07/2025 James Stothard:
The meeting focused on an unexpected requirement to renew a long-standing reseller agreement with Aurea for the Messenger product and the customer- and legal-related implications of that renewal. Participants reviewed that a historic agreement dating from around 2013 had not been flagged as non‑automatically renewing until raised by Tim, and James indicated he expects only minor modernizing updates, has requested inclusion on the legal ticket, and will prepare and circulate an updated reseller agreement for review. Kaupo noted the historic document contains obsolete sections and may require structural updates.

Discussion covered customer impact from Aurea’s floor-price policy, which has pushed several smaller customers toward migration plans while larger, heavily integrated accounts remain stable. Near-term renewals of concern include Axfood and Instru/Instra, with SEB Bank and a Swedish municipality cited as stable accounts and the Central Bank of Estonia identified as at risk. The group also evaluated Messenger’s position: it is an older product not targeted for new sales but still valuable for large, integration‑heavy customers, and recent development work has begun to address earlier degradation on legacy Java releases that previously constrained renewals.

Action Items:
-Kaupo will investigate and try to determine the rationale behind Instru’s one‑year renewal decision and report back
-Kaupo will activate both Axfood and Instru accounts and assess their migration status during February
-James will be added to the legal ticket for the reseller agreement and follow the ticket updates
-James will share draft reseller/distributor agreement updates with Kaupo when available
-James will assemble and send the reseller agreement to Kaupo as soon as possible
-James will check with the legal team about required updates to the reseller agreement
-James will send renewal details for the July renewals to Kaupo (if needed)
-Kaupo will get feedback from the Axfood/customer team regarding the renewal quotations before proceeding

Name: Intuit Inc. - Khoros - Renewal - 1706058 - 7_2026
Churn_Risks__c: Product issues;Support issues;Loss of trust in product/company
NextStep: 04/07/2026 Sebastian Desand: Follow-up email asking customer to schedule a call. If no response, I will ask BU to reach out to the customer again.
Description:
04/07/2026 Sebastian Desand: Follow-up email asking customer to schedule a call. If no response, I will ask BU to reach out to the customer again. 

04/07/2026 Sebastian Desand: USAGE AND HEALTH REPORT FROM THE BU: 

SUMMARY: 
The renewal landscape for Intuit is defined by strong platform usage and a pragmatic customer sentiment that prioritizes stability over vision. While significant technical hurdles—including a pending community ingestion outage, data migration gaps, and a P0 security ticket—remain top-of-mind for stakeholders, high-volume engagement across QuickBooks and TurboTax suggests a healthy ecosystem. Intuit is not looking for "AI for AI's sake" but rather a transparent support motion and a clear path to resolving recurring friction points. Ultimately, the renewal strategy should focus on reinforcing trust through operational excellence, as the account shows no signs of usage decline despite some modest month-over-month volume fluctuations.

Renewal Key Drivers
-Stability & Reliability: Recent outages and high-priority security tickets mean the renewal conversation must lead with platform uptime and incident resolution.
-Operational Confidence: Gaps in data migration and export completeness remain a live concern regarding the platform's execution quality and data portability.
-Support Transparency: Improving visibility into case management and escalation paths is critical to resolving recurring friction with the Intuit team.
-Healthy Usage Metrics: Despite slight dips in large community volumes, the Response and Care environments show steady growth (up to +9% MoM) and strong engagement.
-Pragmatic Partnership: The customer is actively evaluating "fit" and ecosystem integration, requiring a grounded narrative focused on practical outcomes rather than broad AI trends.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION: 

1. Recent outage and whether confidence is fully restored.
A very recent urgent issue was “Community Posts not ingesting into Khoros Care.” One related case was closed on Apr 5, but another outage ticket is still showing as pending, so renewals should be ready for the customer to reference this incident even if the technical work is mostly done. This matters less as a support detail and more as a trust and reliability topic in the renewal conversation.

2. Open support issues that could color the renewal discussion.
There are still active support cases that may come up, especially Facebook DM images not showing in Khoros Care and robots.txt updates for the TTLC community. There is also a recent P0 cross-site scripting ticket for ttlc.intuit.com, which is the kind of issue stakeholders remember even if it is being handled. The renewal team should know which open tickets are still unresolved and be ready to speak to status, ownership, and expected path to closure.

3. Data export / migration confidence is a live customer concern.
The export thread is one of the clearest operational themes in recent customer communication. Intuit raised gaps around exported data structure and completeness — including things like title/description fields, visibility scope, attachments, inline images, avatars, and format expectations. Even though the latest response suggested the data looked good at a high level and Khoros could proceed, this is still a renewal-relevant confidence topic because it touches portability, trust, and execution quality.

4. QBR sentiment was constructive, but clearly pragmatic and not blindly enthusiastic.
In the March QBR, Intuit’s tone came across as engaged but demanding. They emphasized stability, personalization, and practical AI use, with explicit interest in outcomes rather than “AI for AI’s sake.” That suggests the renewal conversation should stay grounded in what has improved, what is reliable now, and what remaining gaps are being addressed, rather than leaning too heavily on broad vision messaging.

5. Product stability is a central narrative they care about.
The QBR discussion specifically highlighted platform stability as a major topic, and Khoros leadership positioned recent work as targeted investments that improved stability after the acquisition. That means stability is not just a support issue — it is part of the customer’s broader evaluation framework. Renewals should be prepared to connect recent incident handling, support responsiveness, and platform improvements into one coherent story.

6. Support experience and case management visibility have been recurring friction points.
Across TAM syncs, there is a repeated theme around tracking support cases, visibility into closed cases, prioritization, and escalation clarity. Even when there are not many escalations, the customer appears to care about how transparently issues are managed. For renewal, this means the team should be ready to discuss not only product performance, but also whether the customer feels they have a clear, responsive support motion.

7. Competitive / fit evaluation is still in the background.
The QBR included discussion of decision factors, fit gaps, and what Intuit still needs to see, which suggests this is not a purely administrative renewal. The customer appears to be evaluating whether the platform is meeting their strategic needs, especially around support-channel effectiveness, ecosystem integration, and practical innovation. Renewals should treat this as a conversation about continued fit and confidence, not just continuation of service.

Intuit QuickBooks Response
Usage looks steady to improving. Outgoing activity is 2,059 this month, incoming is 15,031, and MoM is +9% with a 14% response rate. This does not look like a decline story; it looks like a healthy, active environment with modest improvement.

Intuit QuickBooks Community
This one shows a small month-over-month decline at -8%, but it is still marked healthy and remains very large in absolute volume at 1.28M outgoing. On its own, this looks more like normal fluctuation than a worrying drop, especially since YoY is strongly positive.

Intuit TurboTax Community
This is the biggest visible usage base and it is also showing a moderate MoM decline at -11%. Even so, volume is still extremely high at 5.3M outgoing, health is healthy, and YoY growth is very strong. This is worth noting, but not something that by itself suggests adoption risk.

Intuit Inc. (TurboTax) Response
This looks strong and healthy. Outgoing is 35,500, incoming is 85,555, response rate is 41%, and MoM is +9%. This is one of the strongest usage signals in the account and supports a stable renewal story rather than a decline concern

Intuit Inc. – Pro Community
Usage appears stable to slightly improving with +5% MoM and 289,662 outgoing. Nothing here suggests weakening engagement. This looks like a healthy, steady product footprint.

Intuit Experts Community
This is a small-volume environment, but it is trending upward at +10% MoM. Because the base is tiny (297 outgoing), I would not over-interpret it, but there is no decline signal here.

Intuit PCG Care
This instance is small but stable/improving, with 494 outgoing, 4,338 incoming, 11% response rate, and +8% MoM. No obvious usage concern here; if it comes up, the story is steady rather than deteriorating.

Intuit Community Platform (intuitedu)
This one is basically flat, with +0% MoM and low volume (1,583 outgoing). That suggests stable but limited activity, not decline.

Overall takeaway
There is no broad usage decline across the account. The only visible softening is in the two largest Community instances — QuickBooks Community (-8%) and TurboTax Community (-11%) — but both remain healthy and very large in scale. The Response/Care environments are mostly stable or improving, so usage data does not currently point to a major renewal risk. If the renewals team mentions usage, the right framing is: overall steady-to-healthy, with some modest month-over-month softening in large community volumes but nothing that looks like a sharp adoption problem.

Description:
04-13-2026 Fredrik Scheike: The meeting reviewed the upcoming Kayako renewal, current product usage, and consolidation planning across a 21-company corporate group. Piotr confirmed he is covering Joe’s responsibilities during Joe’s transition toward retirement and that he has downloaded a support data dump for backup and compliance; the broader consolidation decision is expected around August. The group’s operational notes included daily Kayako use, intermittent resolvable issues, and an SLA attainment of about 91%, plus limited AI adoption and use of an out-of-system knowledge base.

Discussion of commercial terms clarified that Kayako requires a minimum 12-month contract and also offers three- and five-year terms, with multi-year price locks avoiding planned increases but monthly contracts unavailable. The buyer signaled a preference for a 12-month renewal and set an internal decision deadline of 17 May for a July renewal. Fredrik described Kayako’s Prime program and available credits (including an AWS cost optimizer) and offered follow-up materials. The parties agreed to a 15-minute follow-up check-in proposed for Monday, May 4 at 14:00 UK time, and the seller committed to schedule the call, update recipient emails, send the 12-month quote, and provide a written summary.

02-25-2026 Fredrik Scheike: Fredrik and Karl discussed upcoming DNN renewals and account status updates. Fredrik confirmed he will be the sole renewal contact going forward. National College of Ireland will discontinue its paid license due to cost but remain on the DNN open-source version. One DPD license (July) will be discontinued, while the September DPD license is expected to renew. RCPI will continue, and PrepayPower is expected to renew for now but may downgrade in the future. Karl also asked about a previously offered early-renewal incentive that allowed clients to lock in current pricing if renewed six months in advance; Fredrik will check internally and revert.

Karl reiterated that pricing—particularly the 25% annual increases—is the primary reason for churn in the Irish market, estimating 8–10 clients have downgraded to open-source DNN or churned altogether over the years. Despite this, he emphasized they remain invested in DNN, using it extensively for bespoke, application-heavy implementations. Fredrik committed to try and get updated pricing sheets for tendering from IgniteTech and to bring forth the structured pricing feedback internally.

Description:
04/05/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent Hans-Jürgen (cc Thomas, Ferdinand) a commercial summary of each party's contractual obligations and rights at contract expiry under the CloudSense Platform Agreement (signed 21-Apr-2023), covering system access, data, backups, audit rights, usage records, software copies, and outstanding payments. Noted this is not a legal opinion.

04/01/2026 Tim Courtenay: Replied to Hans-Jürgen's proposed meeting slots (tomorrow 12:00–12:30, 15:30–16:00; Friday 10:30–11:00) confirming tomorrow 12:00–12:30 works and asking him to send the meeting invite.

03/20/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent an end-of-week check-in to Hans-Jürgen and Thomas asking for an update on their renewal thinking and next steps, and proposed a brief call early next week.

03/16/2026 Tim Courtenay: Resent the standalone renewal quote to Hans-Jürgen and Thomas. Asked them to confirm when they want to discuss next steps.

03/16/26 SYSTEM - Quote Q-47882 is out for signature, moving the opportunity to the Quote Follow-Up stage.

03/16/2026 SYSTEM - Quote Q-43769 is Rejected. Approver Comments  -  Quote rejected - superseded by Q-47882

03/16/2026 SYSTEM - Quote Q-47882 is Accepted. Approver Comments  -  Quote approved

03/12/2026 Tim Courtenay: Advised Hans-Jürgen and Thomas that the co-term renewal quote covering both subscriptions (main renewal + Orchestrator Accelerator, both expiring 30 Apr 2027) will be sent today via AdobeSign from Carlos Encalada. Co-terming has resulted in a slightly reduced cost vs. renewing separately.

03/11/2026 Tim Courtenay: Confirmed co-terming of the Orchestrator Accelerator (Aug) renewal with the main renewal, both terminating 30 Apr 2027, resulting in a cost saving for A1. Combined quote to be sent tomorrow.

03/10/2026 Tim Courtenay: SVP has approved the co-terming of the two opportunities, with the end dates being co-termed to opp 006Ih000003iO6JIAU, i.e. 4/30/2027. Details:
- opp 006Ih000003iO6JIAU renewal will run from 5/1/2026 to 4/30/2027, amount = EUR 450,033.53 * 1.25 = EUR 562,541.91.
- this opp will run from 8/1/2026 to 4/30/2027, amount = EUR 25,575.00 *1.25 * 9/12 = EUR 23,976.56.
Sales Ops to create a co-termed quote and update the opportunity names etc.

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Submitting quote for approval as the fallback option in the event that co-terming is not agreed. Quote detail details are as follows:
Term: 1 yr
Standard success, 25% uplift
ARR = EUR 25,575.00 * 1.25 = EUR 31,968.72
Recipient: Hans-Jurgen Adlassnig.
Standard terms and conditions.
Select selected option to send the quote to the opportunity owner, so that I can send to legal to add the clause regarding the version support policy.

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay: Emailed Todd and Nadir at Skyvera to discuss preferred renewal structure — Scenario B, €586,518.42 total), rather than our standard approach of extending both to August 2027 (Scenario A, €735,146.07). Scenario A deemed unviable given A1's cost pressure. Realistic choice is Scenario B vs. no co-term (Scenario C, €594,510.60).

03/09/2026 Tim Courtenay:   Renewal follow-up call with Hans-Juergen Adlassnig, Thomas Lehner, and Tim Courtenay regarding the upcoming Skyvera-managed CloudSense renewal for A1 Telekom Austria. A1 challenged the 12-month renewal price of EUR 562,000 versus current spend of EUR 450,000, requested clarity on the separate Orchestrator Accelerator renewal, asked for coterm alignment to reduce complexity/cost, and made clear that lower usage, limited internal adoption, and a wider strategic review are driving a very cautious renewal stance. Customer feedback was that the current static commercial position risks damaging the wider relationship and future competitiveness, while Tim set expectations that price movement is unlikely on a one-year deal but would try to improve the coterm treatment and provide a final offer for board review.
Actions:
* Tim Courtenay to send best and final offer by Wednesday covering both subscriptions.
* Tim Courtenay to check whether Orchestrator Accelerator can be co-termed to the main renewal end date.
* Tim Courtenay to escalate customer feedback internally.
* A1 to use final pricing for board discussion next week.
* A1 to decide whether to proceed with the 12-month renewal.

04/12/24 CD: FC w Dilip - says they want to cancel subscription due to breach of contract, passed over to VP to engage BU to discuss, notes from call:

Key points:

 - renewal is due 2026 but they are asking to cancel early since they believe we are in breach of contract which all stems from a broken plug in

 - they use the software for a contract they have with Airbus, Airbus had provided a plug in which did some kind of automation for them (I did not get into the specifics) 

 - when they upgraded to  the new version last year this plugin stopped working.  They also mentioned that they cannot revert to the previous version as the edition they use is dictated by Airbus.  

 - I've pushed back on this making the point that since Airbus provided the plugin for the previous version, surely they should be responsible to providing a plugin that is compatible with the new version

 - they said they raised this with Airbus and Airbus said it's not an issue with the plugin but an issue with the Autotrol software that needs to be fixed

Customer is looking for technical assistance to get this resolved and says if we cannot fix it then they will need to look at an alternative solution which they would prefer to avoid doing if possible.

Not sure if there is anything we can do here but it would be great if someone could pick it up and advise the customer either way.  With the technical issue and the contractual implications I thought this might need input from support and the BU.

03/24/2026 James Quigley: Summary:

The meeting reviewed Quest's use of Acorn and renewal options for the upcoming contract period. James Quigley and Paul discussed that Quest primarily uses Acorn to calculate testing costs, and Paul was tasked with obtaining one-year and three-year renewal quotes to inform the decision. They reviewed support tiers: standard support and a platinum tier that includes a managed upgrade covering the technical migration. Paul reported the managed upgrade work has not been completed and the current installation remains on a 2013 version, with technical difficulties hindering progress.

The conversation covered contract timing and procurement logistics, noting the current contract expires in July and decisions will be made in the months leading up to that date. James committed to sending pricing for both standard and platinum support along with explanatory materials, to verify whether the vendor legal/entity information needs updating, and to provide updated procurement paperwork, including a W-9 if required. No final procurement decision was recorded during the discussion.

04-13-2026 Fredrik Scheike_ The meeting was an initial renewal discussion for Allo’s Kayako subscription where the Trilogy renewals manager introduced himself, confirmed call recording, explained Trilogy’s role in supporting multiple product lines, and outlined the agenda covering renewal-term and price-lock options, Standard versus Platinum success plans, and Prime program credits. The vendor confirmed terms remain unchanged from last year, including the 60-day cancellation window before the renewal date of August 28, noted recently added AI-driven Kayako features, and reviewed last year’s spend and typical lift rates to frame one-, three-, and five-year price-lock options with annual payment scheduling.

Allo attendees identified themselves as IT administrators who manage Kayako from a technical/admin perspective and explained they inherited the product roughly five years ago. Paul said he could not commit to multi-year terms without consulting his forecasting and manager because his forecasting horizon is about 16–18 months. Fredrik clarified that quotes are non-binding until signed, offered to produce a three-year quote and a feature/service-level comparison, and proposed follow-up dates in May. The group agreed to reconvene on May 25, Fredrik offered to send the Renewals portal link and collateral after the call, and the vendor restated the final decision deadline of June 28.

NextStep: CLOSED WON. Customer has signed the quoyte. We need to sign. 04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay
Description:
04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Customer returned the signed quote (ref Case 00031904), looks like Artie still needs to sign it.

04/12/2026 Tim Courtenay: Replied to Jean-Francoys confirming that legal has been asked to send the fully executed quote, and that the auto-renewal clause from the previous contract will not apply given the new quote has been signed — no separate notice required.

I asked for legal to check they have received the signed quote.

04/10/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Customer wants a 3-yr ramped pricing quote. We need to get this out ASAP - today is the day they will approve and hopefully sign. Creating Case to get that approved. Using Q-47040 as the starting quote, produced redline for legal approval. It is going to be challenging to get this executed today - asked Jean-Francoys what the fallback plan is if we don't get it done today (given that he is retiring to a non-exec position from next week).

04/08/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent final 3yr renewal offer to Jean-Francoys (CEO, Outbox Technologies). After a call the prior day, presented a ramped pricing alternative to the flat 3yr quote — same TCV of $718,922.88 but front-loaded relief in Year 1 ($213,951 vs. $239,641 flat), stepping up ~10.6% annually. Noted a 4yr term could have yielded a more meaningful rate reduction but was off the table. Asked Jean-Francoys to confirm interest to proceed with a formal quote.

04/07/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Renewal call with Jean-Francoys Brousseau (Outbox Technologies) to discuss upcoming Onyx renewal. Customer is internally split between 1-year and 3-year term; Jean-Francoys is retiring at the end of this week. He prefers 3 years but team favors 1 year. Product still valued for ad hoc use but usage is declining. Customer requested pricing flexibility to secure a 3-year agreement before his retirement this week; otherwise likely outcome is a 1-year renewal handled by team later. Tested the appetite for a flat 4, he said that would not be possible. Maximum term is 3 yrs.
Actions:
* Tim to confirm internally whether pricing flexibility can be offered (urgent)
* Tim to revert to customer within 1–2 days
* Customer to potentially sign 3-year deal this week if pricing acceptable

Note that the HVO playbook does not allow a discounted 3 yr renewal. Customer cannot consider 4 yr renewal, so there is no pricing option other than ramped pricing over the 3 yrs.

03/30/26 SYSTEM - Quote Q-48031 is out for signature, moving the opportunity to the Quote Follow-Up stage.

03/30/2026 SYSTEM - Quote Q-47040 is Accepted. Approver Comments  -  3yr quote approved - David M 03/30/2026

03/30/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Submitted 1yr and 3yr quotes for approval.

03/30/2026 Tim Courtenay: Followed up on the SSO action item from last week's meeting. Confirmed internally that SSO has been delivered for other customers and is achievable, but sits outside the core product roadmap and would require a bespoke Professional Services engagement. Advised that fixed-cost pricing isn't possible upfront — scoping would be needed first. Offered to facilitate a scoping conversation if they want to proceed.

03/25/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Asked Jaime (AM) about the feasibility of incorporating SSO as a paid-for PS engagement. When I have this info I will send the quotes and explanatory email.

03/24/2026 Tim Courtenay: Renewal call with Outbox Technologies (Jean-François Brousseau, Ken Pope, JP) regarding Onyx. Customer has largely phased out Onyx from core operations, now using it minimally for basic account management and occasional features. They have built an internal replacement and are gradually retiring Onyx. Key concerns include lack of product investment, security limitations (no SSO/2FA), and poor value from support. Renewal decision will be highly price-driven, with customer weighing cost vs. remaining utility.
Actions:
- Trilogy to send renewal quote by end of week
- Customer to review and respond
- Customer to search for original license agreement

Description:
04/14/2026 Tim Courtenay: Replied to Diego to clarify the purpose of the package mapping exercise — it is not about pricing validation. Explained that renewal pricing applies a standard policy-based uplift with no per-module breakdown; the message to the customer is simply "you continue with what you have." Redirected Diego to the original request from Shai (CoreSite) on 11/25/2025: cross-reference CoreSite's installed packages against Skyvera's product set, validate compatibility, and identify version gaps. Provided Diego with the current contract entitlements (135 Full Users, 115 Light Users, 115 Orchestrator Users; modules: CPQ, Configurator, OSM, Process Orchestration, Order Enrichment; 1 production org, 5 sandboxes; pricing at total contract level).

04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Marked Leah Miles as the PC. 

NOTE: QUOTE MUST NOT BE SENT UNTIL WE HAVE CLARITY ON THE APPLICABILITY OF THE MSA PRICE CAP CLAUSE.

04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay: Emailed Leah, Alex, and Shai to clarify whether the billing/shipping name should be updated to "CoreSite L.P." on future quotes. Noted that legal review of the current contract is pending (expected within 2 days), which is holding up finalization of renewal quotes. Proposed a follow-up meeting next week to cover renewal offer review, contract requirements, license matrix status, and next steps, with a link to book directly in calendar.

04/13/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Requested the legal case to be processed - we need to know if there is a toxic price cap or not. I followed up internally with Diego Parima on the module mapping request information that is still outstanding. I need this before reverting to the customer.

04/08/2026 Tim Courtenay:  Leah Miles provided the updated W-9.

04/07/2026 Tim Courtenay: Renewal call with Coresite (Diego Parima, Alex K, Leah Mi) and Skyvera (Tim Courtenay, Christian Mejia) to discuss upcoming CloudSense renewal and migration status. Customer is migrating to another CPQ tool but is not confident they will complete this before the September renewal, and requested options for a potential extension. Agreed to reassess after internal milestone in June while continuing renewal discussions and information sharing in parallel.
Actions:
* Coresite to share module mapping analysis, ship-to address update, and W-9
* Coresite to confirm legal requirements (novation or other documentation)
* Tim to verify MSA price cap and provide renewal options
* Tim to confirm contract terms (new vs existing) and share templates if applicable
* Both parties to stay engaged ahead of June milestone

Shai sent an email with the module mapping, which was previously shared with Kevin Kuch.
Sent follow up email to the CoreSite team.
Requested Neeraj's assistance in mapping the licenses from customer data to ours.

04/06/2026 Tim Courtenay: Emailed Leah (cc: Aleksandra) regarding CoreSite's CloudSense (now Skyvera) renewal ahead of the Sep 5, 2026 expiry. Key points raised: (1) vendor registration must be updated to reflect the Skyvera entity transition; (2) renewal details need to be aligned — MSA update, pricing, options, and version support; (3) their deployment is substantial (CPQ, Order Management, Process Orchestration, Orchestrator) and a lapse would have operational impact. Proposed a call this week or next and shared a calendar link.

03/30/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent a follow-up to Leah (CC: Aleksandra) nudging on the earlier intro email and reiterating the request to book a time via the Calendly link to kick off the CloudSense renewal. 

03/25/2026 Tim Courtenay: Sent intro email to Leah (and Aleksandra) at Coresite following Neeraj's introduction, confirming my role in the CloudSense renewal. Outlined three agenda items for an initial check-in call and shared a Calendly link for scheduling.

03/26/2026 James Quigley: 

Summary:

The meeting opened as an introductory renewal discussion for AlertFind (MessageOne) ahead of the September 16 renewal date and focused on operational stability, functionality, and commercial questions from NTT. NTT described recent incidents where SMS messages failed or were delayed and noted slow or delayed support responses routed through an AI assistant; the group asked whether there have been product enhancements, version changes, or planned Gen AI integrations to address these issues. Participants also sought clearer pricing, discount structures for an existing customer, and demonstrations of complementary products or workflows that could reduce manual effort.

James Quigley, the vendor representative, acknowledged the concerns and said he would escalate them to product and support teams and follow up with detailed answers and pricing ahead of a scheduled follow-up meeting. He explained the current legal and support structure—MessageOne LLC operating under Trilogy/Jigsaw/ESW Operations—and outlined a "prime program" option that can bundle two additional products, Contently and Kayako, potentially at no additional cost. Outstanding items include the vendor's detailed technical responses, definitive pricing, and any demos for complementary products.
